summary: Base64-decoded payload executed via eval() at module load
details: router_init.js line 4 contains a Buffer.from(<...>, "base64").toString(...) expression piped directly into eval(), which decodes and executes an obfuscated payload when the module is loaded. This pattern hides the actual code from review and lets the maintainer ship arbitrary behavior to installers (credential theft, exfiltration, dropper, persistence) without it being visible in the published source. There is no legitimate reason for a helper/test utility to base64-decode-and-eval at load time; the construction itself is the attack indicator. Any project that installs and requires this package will execute the hidden payload in the installer's Node.js process.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
